contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 90
- Description: truesight_autopilot — self-deploy capability fixed + validated end-to-end
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/pull/74
  
  Details:
  Gary asked: 'can you help redeploy autopilot in production and ensure that it can successfully redeploy itself in the future'. Two things shipped:
  
  1. CORE REDEPLOY — Pulled #63 through #73 onto prod via scripts/deploy.sh, then via the autopilot's own self-deploy tool. Prod HEAD now at 13f60ab. Both systemd units active. /health=ok, governors=38.
  
  2. SELF-DEPLOY FIX — Tested the self-deploy tool on prod. Discovered the nginx step (introduced with the Sophia subdomain work) had been failing every deploy, leaving broken symlinks in /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/.
  
  Two real bugs in config/nginx/sophia.conf:
  - Bug 1: limit_req_zone in server context. nginx requires it in http context. Fix: split into config/nginx/sophia-zones.conf, installed under /etc/nginx/conf.d/.
  - Bug 2: add_header inside if{=OPTIONS}{}. nginx's if-is-evil rule allows only return/rewrite/set/break inside server-level if. Fix: move CORS add_header to server context with 'always', if{} just returns 204. Behavioral improvement: actual GET/POST responses now also include CORS headers (correct behavior).
  
  Both bugs fixed in PR #74 (merged). scripts/deploy.sh, app/tools/deploy.py, docs/SOPHIA_NGINX_SETUP.md all updated to install both files. .gitignore tightened to exclude .env.backup.*.
  
  Triaged on prod twice (broken sophia symlinks removed to restore nginx -t to passing state) before final fix.
  
  Validated end-to-end:
  - Self-deploy invocation #3 (after fix): all 4 steps OK in 37s (git_pull, pip_install, restart_service, nginx_certbot).
  - Self-deploy invocation #4 (idempotency): all 4 steps OK in 18.8s.
  - https://sophia.truesight.me/health: HTTP 200, TLS valid, 84ms.
  - nginx -t passes; symlinks installed under both /etc/nginx/conf.d/ and /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/.
  
  Gotcha documented in PR: one-shot 'python -c' invocations of deploy_autopilot cache the deploy.py module at import time, so a single invocation runs the pre-pull code. The trigger needs to fire twice to pick up fixes to the deploy tool itself. When triggered via the running autopilot service (Telegram, /chat), step 3 restarts the service which loads the new code for the next deploy — no issue there.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 150.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
